title: Cobblemon Epsilon
description: Cobblemon Delta works pretty fine as a standalone modpack, but lacking a few features we like.
body:
Cobblemon Delta works pretty fine as a standalone modpack, but lacking a few features me and my friends likes to play with, so I just made this for easier access. Ignore the license for now, I still need to comb through all the mods and datapacks to see if what license I maybe required. 

## Extra QoL compare to Cobblemon Delta (Too lazy to copy for now)
- Smelt Rotten Flesh into Leather
- Uncraft slabs and stairs
- Wool into string
- Mounting Pokemons
- Legendary Spawns
- Cobblemon items in dungeon loots
- Grave stone
- Fortunable Evo Stone
- Anti mob grief (need to fix creeper doing no damage)
- Guarantee double Shulker Shells
- Faster leaf decay
- AFK display
- Cauldron concrete
- /trigger home/sethome/back
- More mob heads
- Silence mob tags
- Unlock all recipes
- TPA

# To do
- ~~Separate Client and Server mods~~
- Figure out the license
- ~~Find out why there are 2 Cobblemon mods, and see which one can be nuked~~
- ~~Figure out how to automate the modpack creation process, instead of manually editing the Json file~~

# Licenses
- Durability Tooltip is the property of SuperMartijn642 and is protected under copyright law and may not be altered or reuploaded without direct permission from SuperMartijn642. However, we can still use it in modpacks without any issue, so shoutout to him. 
- Xaero's World Map's creator allows us to use their mods in modpacks, with the only condition being if this modpack is distributed outside Modrinth and CurseForge we will need to give him a shoutout, so here is a link to the [original mod page](https://modrinth.com/mod/xaeros-world-map) along with the [mod page for the minimap](https://modrinth.com/mod/xaeros-minimap)
- Better Advancements' creator way2muchnoise uses their own [DBAJ License](https://github.com/way2muchnoise/BetterAdvancements/blob/master/LICENSE.md), which is worth quite a read if you ask me
- Complementary Shaders - Reimagined uses their [own license](https://github.com/ComplementaryDevelopment/ComplementaryReimagined/blob/main/License.txt) which gives me a bit of a scare
-
